title: Day 1: Typography 101
content: Theory: Anatomy of type, Serif vs. Sans-Serif, and the emotion of fonts.
exercise: Exercise: Choose 3 global brands. Identify their font style and write 2 sentences on why that font fits their "personality."

title: Day 2: Color Theory
content: Theory: Harmonies, contrast, and psychological triggers of color.
exercise: Exercise: Create 3 distinct color palettes (5 colors each) for: a Luxury Spa, a Cyberpunk Game, and a Kids' Toy Store.

title: Day 3: Grid Systems
content: Theory: How to use columns and gutters to create order and balance.
exercise: Exercise: Draw a 12-column grid on paper. Sketch 3 different ways to arrange a headline, an image, and a button on that grid.

title: Day 4: Visual Hierarchy
content: Theory: Guiding the eye. Using scale, weight, and color to show the viewer what to look at first.
exercise: Exercise: Redesign a crowded event flyer. Ensure the "Benefit/Hook" is seen first, and the "Date/Location" second.

title: Day 5: White Space
content: Theory: The power of "Negative Space." Why breathing room increases a design's value.
exercise: Exercise: Take a cluttered social media post. Remove 30% of the elements and rearrange the rest to focus on a single message.

title: Day 6: Contrast & Repetition
content: Theory: Making elements stand out vs. making them feel like a family.
exercise: Exercise: Design 3 different website buttons that use different colors but share the exact same shape and "feel."

title: Day 7: The Visual Audit
content: Theory: Deconstructing professional design to understand the "logic."
exercise: Exercise: Find a world-class ad. List the specific fonts, primary/secondary colors, and the type of grid it uses.

title: Day 8: Vector vs. Raster
content: Theory: Why logos must be vectors (Illustrator/Figma) and photos are rasters (Photoshop).
exercise: Exercise: Open any design tool. Export a simple circle as a PNG and an SVG. Zoom in 1000% on both and note the difference.

title: Day 9: The Pen Tool Mastery
content: Theory: Anchor points, paths, and handles. The fundamental skill of vector drawing.
exercise: Exercise: Find a logo with curves (like Apple or Twitter). Use the Pen Tool to trace the outline perfectly.

title: Day 10: Shape Builder Tool
content: Theory: Creating complex shapes from simple circles and squares.
exercise: Exercise: Create a complex icon (e.g., a "Cloud" or a "House") using only 5 basic geometric shapes and the "Unite" or "Exclude" functions.

title: Day 11: Layers & Groups
content: Theory: Organizing a professional "Kitchen." Naming and grouping so others can edit your work.
exercise: Exercise: Build a composite of 10 items. Every layer must be named, and groups must be logically color-coded.

title: Day 12: Selection & Masking
content: Theory: "Non-destructive" editing. Removing backgrounds and hiding parts of an image without deleting pixels.
exercise: Exercise: Cut a person out of a photo and place them on a clean, solid background. Use a mask, not an eraser.

title: Day 13: Brushes & Textures
content: Theory: Adding "soul" to flat designs. Using grit and grain to create a premium, tactile feel.
exercise: Exercise: Take a flat circle and use a texture brush to make it look like a 3D, weathered piece of metal.

title: Day 14: Typography Layouts
content: Theory: Kerning (letter space) and Leading (line space).
exercise: Exercise: Format a paragraph of text. Adjust the leading and kerning until it looks professional and is incredibly easy to read.

title: Day 15: Logo Symbolism
content: Theory: Creating marks with meaning. The difference between a "symbol" and an "illustration."
exercise: Exercise: Sketch 10 tiny "thumbnail" concepts for a logo for a company called "Swift Delivery."

title: Day 16: Brand Identity Boards
content: Theory: Building a visual ecosystem. Logo + Fonts + Colors.
exercise: Exercise: Create a 1-page "Brand Board" for a fictional tech startup. Show how the fonts and colors complement the logo.

title: Day 17: Social Carousels
content: Theory: Designing for "The Swipe." Creating seamless transitions between slides.
exercise: Exercise: Design 5 carousel slides in Figma. Ensure an image from Slide 1 "bleeds" over into Slide 2.

title: Day 18: YouTube Thumbnails
content: Theory: High-contrast design for tiny screens. Why faces and big text get clicks.
exercise: Exercise: Design a thumbnail for a video titled "How to Rich." Use a bold face and high-contrast text.

title: Day 19: Poster & Flyer Design
content: Theory: Combining all Week 1 principles into a large-scale layout.
exercise: Exercise: Design a vertical poster for a local music festival. Focus on a clear hierarchy of information.

title: Day 20: Ad Banner Design
content: Theory: Action-oriented design. Making the "Button" the hero of the ad.
exercise: Exercise: Create a square "Sale" ad. The button must be the most prominent visual element after the headline.

title: Day 21: Presentation Design
content: Theory: Designing for data. How to make charts and lists look professional and clear.
exercise: Exercise: Design 3 slides: a Title slide, a "Stats" slide with a bar chart, and a "Meet the Team" slide.

title: Day 22: UI Basics (Mobile)
content: Theory: Designing for fingers. Proper button sizes and spacing for mobile apps.
exercise: Exercise: In Figma, design a simple "Sign Up" screen for a fitness app.

title: Day 23: UX (User Experience)
content: Theory: Flow and accessibility. How a user moves through a digital product.
exercise: Exercise: Map out the "User Flow" (Step 1, Step 2, Step 3) for buying a ticket on a cinema app.

title: Day 24: Real-world Mockups
content: Theory: Presenting your work. How to place your art on real-life objects using templates.
exercise: Exercise: Take your Day 15 logo and place it onto a high-quality mockup of a coffee cup or a building.

title: Day 25: Print Design (CMYK)
content: Theory: Bleeds, margins, and the CMYK vs. RGB color space.
exercise: Exercise: Set up a document for a business card. Include a 0.125-inch "Bleed" and set the color mode to CMYK.

title: Day 26: The Revision Process
content: Theory: Dealing with feedback. How to fix a design without losing its core quality.
exercise: Exercise: Take your Day 19 poster. Imagine a client said "Make the text bigger." Do it without breaking the hierarchy.

title: Day 27: Speed Challenge
content: Theory: Efficiency under pressure. Making fast decisions without sacrificing quality.
exercise: Exercise: Set a timer for 20 minutes. Design a logo for a "Pizza Place" from scratch.

title: Day 28: Portfolio Selection
content: Theory: Quality over quantity. Choosing the "Big 3" that show your range.
exercise: Exercise: Select your 3 best projects from the month. Write a 2-sentence "Goal" and "Solution" for each.

title: Day 29: Portfolio Presentation
content: Theory: Storytelling your work. How to show the "Process," not just the final result.
exercise: Exercise: Upload your Big 3 to a Behance profile. Include a screenshot of your "Rough Sketches" for each.

title: Day 30: Final Review
content: Theory: The "Expert" polish. Fixing the small details that 99% of people miss.
exercise: Exercise: Find a design from Week 1. Redesign it using everything you learned in Week 4.
